steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on both sides of the blank
steps.content:
The left side is an independent clause: “reflects the combined efforts and expertise of multiple [researchers]” — a complete thought.

The right side, “Jesus Rivera is listed first, Maryam Sadat Hosseini, David Restrepo, and other coauthors also made important contributions to the study,” provides specific information about who these researchers are and how they contributed.

steps.title: Apply the rule: colon introduces elaboration, while creates the supporting relationship
steps.content:
A colon signals that what follows elaborates on or explains the main clause. Here, the elaboration answers the implicit question: Which researchers? The word while (meaning whereas) then breaks down the composition of these multiple researchers—showing that Jesus Rivera is listed first and other coauthors contributed too, which justifies calling them multiple researchers.

Option A (semicolon) treats the second clause as equally independent, not elaborative. Option B (comma + while) lacks the emphasis that elaboration deserves. Option C (period) unnecessarily separates these closely related ideas.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
